codeReview.summary: The fix resolves the original SC/TC length mismatch, switches production off the stale static zh-TW snapshot, localizes the Continue button class names, and moves the two help-panel control labels into locale data. However, the new runtime zh-TW path still applies a blind character-by-character conversion to every zh-CN string, and that produces incorrect Traditional Chinese in visible UI text (for example `钢铁意志,剑盾无双` becomes `鋼鐵意誌,劍盾無雙`).

issues.description: `generateZhTW()` converts every zh-CN value character-by-character with no phrase-level overrides, so the fix still emits invalid Traditional Chinese for context-sensitive words. A visible example is `src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts:33`: `menu.classSelect.warrior.desc` is `钢铁意志,剑盾无双`, which the current runtime converter turns into `鋼鐵意誌,劍盾無雙`; Traditional Chinese should be `鋼鐵意志,劍盾無雙`. That means the fix still fails the mission requirement for valid zh-TW UI text (VAL-FOUND-016 / VAL-MENU-018).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared i18n architecture doc is now stale: it still documents a checked-in `src/i18n/locales/zh-TW.ts` file and says locale data is imported statically, but this fix deleted that file and moved zh-TW generation into `src/i18n/index.ts` at runtime.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:137` still lists `zh-TW.ts`, and `.factory/library/architecture.md:150` says locale data is imported statically; the reviewed fix commit deletes `src/i18n/locales/zh-TW.ts` and `src/i18n/index.ts:19-29` now generates zh-TW from zh-CN at runtime.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `i18n-worker` skill no longer matches repo reality after this fix: it still tells workers to regenerate `zh-TW.ts`, and its example handoff still claims that file is produced, even though zh-TW is now runtime-generated instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/i18n-worker/SKILL.md:63` says 'Never hand-edit `zh-TW.ts` — always regenerate from zh-CN via the converter', and `.factory/skills/i18n-worker/SKILL.md:73` still describes `src/i18n/locales/zh-TW.ts (auto-generated)`, but the reviewed commit removes that file and uses `src/i18n/index.ts:19-29` for runtime generation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation contract examples still reference snake_case locale keys like `menu.new_game`, while the actual implementation and locale files use camelCase keys like `menu.newGame`. That mismatch can mislead future workers and reviewers during manual validation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `validation-contract.md:12,17,22,47,82` use `menu.new_game`, but `src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts:19` defines `menu.newGame` and the code/tests use that camelCase key.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, shared-state docs, prior failure reports, and commit `ecaeeb4`. The rerun does fix the original length-mismatch, static zh-TW snapshot, Continue-label, and help-key findings, but it still cannot pass because the new runtime zh-TW converter produces incorrect Traditional Chinese in visible menu text such as `鋼鐵意誌,劍盾無雙`.
